channel.title: Genetics on Alan Aw
channel.description: Recent content in Genetics on Alan Aw

item.title: Cleaning and Visualizing 1000 Genomes Data
item.description: In 2008, scientists launched the 1000 Genomes Project to catalogue human genetic variation across the globe. This \(N=2504\) by \(P=84.4 \times 10^6\) dataset (in Phase 3) has spurred the development of models and algorithms to relate our genome to many downstream measurements (like gene expression, transcriptome, epigenome, proteome and phenotypes), as well as to upstream biological processes like human population demography and history. If there is one thing that hinders my progress at analyzing real biological datasets, it is bioinformatics.

item.title: Review of “Who We Are and How We Got Here"
item.description: One of my goals this year is to read more books and to think critically about them. I’ve started a spreadsheet tracking books I’ve read and books I’m reading, and I’m excited to share it here. I just finished reading David Reich’s book on ancient DNA (aDNA) and human history, Reich, David. Who We Are and How We Got Here: Ancient DNA and the new science of the human past.
